preamble: The diphthong /au/ can undergo a fascinating transformation where its vowel-like offglide becomes a consonant. This process, known as consonantalization, turns the /u/ element of the diphthong into a labiodental fricative, which can be either voiced ([v]) or voiceless ([f]), depending on the sounds around it.

examples.note: The offglide of the diphthong /au/ consonantalizes to a voiceless labiodental fricative /f/.

sources: Joseph, B. D. (2025). Phonology (Chapter 5). In The Balkan Languages. Cambridge University Press & Assessment.
